Students need champions - they need to know someone believes in them unconditionally. No matter the innovative pedagogy, the educational buzzwords, or newest ed-tech products, the student-teacher relationship is primary.

This year, I am super excited to be co-creating a class called Democratic Inquiry with my students. Using many skills and systems of thinking we've learned over the last two years together (Design Thinking processes, SMART Goal Setting, real time listening, etc.) (1/3)

they are pursuing their own passion projects and goals that is 100% their own ideas and motivation. Everything about the class (grading, direction, amount of guidance from the facillitator, how often we meet together) is entirely decided and voted on by the class as equals. (2/3)

I have one vote, and each student has one vote. I will be posting about this experience alot- and showing the power of opening the curriculum to full student choice. I will be documenting the process, both successes and failures, so stay tuned for updates!

I'm doing an experiment this week to kickoff a month of intentional usage of technology and curating my online content. I'm starting by turning off my youtube search history, removing shorts / reels, and redirecting youtube to my subscriptions page. Breaking my youtube addiciton!
Thank you @Prigoose for this amazing class on using twitter. It feels amazing to be using this technology with a group of people irl and joining the few who actually create content rather than lurk!!

I just blocked a ton of words from my twitter  - "Trump", "Kamala" "Republicans" "democrats" etc. I am avidly into politics of all sorts. Twitter is a terrible place to have a great conversation about this though, and I can still see content from who I follow.

The vast majority of people associate "technology" with corporations and the way they hope to monopolize our attention (think recent google ruling). Technology can and should be associated with creativity and play. I'm hoping to bring that energy with all tech interactions!

The first goal in doing this is to curate your experience regarding content on the internet. Our hooman brains were not designed to experience this amount of information, and algorithms designed by the corporations to keep us hooked and angry make a bad situation worse.

I worry about being canceled, but I think the real fear is posting and feeling ignored. But now I'm thinking of "Social Media" as a fun dance floor that I can activate with my friends by being silly. And maybe its like dancing alone (imo one of life's greatest pleasures)
